title: glycosmis collina
intro: Glycosmis collina, also known as the small-leaved glycosmis or Chinese boxwood, is a plant species in the family Rutaceae, commonly found in tropical regions of Asia. It has been utilized in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties. The health benefits associated with glycosmis collina include its ability to treat digestive issues, reduce fever, and alleviate respiratory problems. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used in infusions, decoctions, and ointments, and can be consumed as a tea or applied topically to the skin.
